Name: CARE
LongName: The CARE (CAse REport) Guidelines: Consensus-based Clinical Case Reporting Guideline Development
Description: The CARE Guidelines provide a consensus-based reporting framework for clinical case reports to improve completeness, transparency, and usefulness. The checklist outlines essential information to include when reporting individual patient cases in clinical medicine.
Aim: To improve the accuracy, transparency, and completeness of published clinical case reports by providing a standardized checklist of essential reporting elements.

Scope.Coverage: Reports describing the clinical course of an individual patient, including presentation, diagnosis, intervention, and outcomes across medical disciplines.
Scope.Focus: Structured and transparent reporting of patient information, clinical findings, diagnostic assessment, therapeutic interventions, follow-up, outcomes, and patient perspective in single case reports.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not intended for randomized trials (CONSORT), observational analytic studies (STROBE), systematic reviews (PRISMA), or formal case series with comparative statistical analysis.

primary.description: Gagnier JJ, Kienle G, Altman DG, et al. The CARE Guidelines: Consensus-based Clinical Case Reporting Guideline Development. J Clin Epidemiol. 2014;67(1):46–51.

items.item_question: Does the title identify the report as a case report and include the phenomenon of greatest interest?
items.item_question_background: If a case report is not clearly labeled in the title, it may be misclassified or overlooked in indexing and evidence synthesis.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Check whether the title includes the words 'case report' (or equivalent) and specifies the main condition, intervention, or phenomenon of interest.

items.item_question: Are 2–5 key words provided that identify the case and its main topics?
items.item_question_background: Keywords enhance discoverability in databases; insufficient or vague terms limit retrieval and dissemination.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the list of keywords and confirm there are between 2 and 5 that reflect the main condition, intervention, and context.

items.item_question: Does the abstract include a brief introduction summarizing the case’s unique contribution?
items.item_question_background: Without a concise framing of why the case matters, readers cannot quickly judge relevance or novelty.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the opening sentence(s) of the abstract that summarize the background and uniqueness of the case.

items.item_question: Does the abstract describe the patient’s main symptoms and important clinical findings?
items.item_question_background: Key presenting features anchor the diagnostic reasoning; omission obscures clinical context.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the description of the patient’s main symptoms and significant clinical findings from the abstract.

items.item_question: Does the abstract summarize the main diagnoses, therapeutic interventions, and outcomes?
items.item_question_background: Summarizing diagnosis, treatment, and outcomes allows rapid appraisal of clinical trajectory and impact.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements describing the final diagnosis, key interventions, and primary outcomes in the abstract.

items.item_question: Does the abstract conclude with the main lessons or take-away message?
items.item_question_background: A clear take-away prevents overinterpretation and clarifies what clinicians should learn from the case.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the concluding sentence(s) of the abstract that state the primary lesson or implication.

items.item_question: Does the introduction provide background and rationale for reporting the case?
items.item_question_background: Background context justifies why the case is noteworthy and situates it within existing literature.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the paragraph(s) in the introduction that explain the context, prior knowledge, and rationale for presenting the case.

items.item_question: Are patient demographic characteristics described (e.g., age, sex, ethnicity, occupation)?
items.item_question_background: Demographics influence disease prevalence and applicability; omission limits interpretability and generalisability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract patient age, sex, ethnicity (if reported), occupation, and other relevant demographic details.

items.item_question: Are the patient’s main symptoms or chief complaints described?
items.item_question_background: Chief complaints initiate the clinical reasoning pathway; without them, diagnostic logic cannot be reconstructed.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the stated main symptoms or chief complaints at presentation.

items.item_question: Is relevant medical, family, and psychosocial history described?
items.item_question_background: Past history and context shape risk assessment and differential diagnosis; missing history obscures causal interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract relevant past medical history, family history, psychosocial factors, lifestyle, and genetic information if available.

items.item_question: Are significant physical examination findings described?
items.item_question_background: Objective clinical findings validate symptom reports and guide diagnostic assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract key physical examination and other clinically relevant findings.

items.item_question: Is a timeline provided summarizing important dates and events?
items.item_question_background: A timeline clarifies sequence and temporality, which are critical for causal inference and understanding progression.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Identify and extract any timeline table or narrative summarizing symptom onset, diagnosis, interventions, and follow-up.

items.item_question: Are diagnostic methods described (e.g., laboratory tests, imaging, surveys)?
items.item_question_background: Without test descriptions, readers cannot assess diagnostic validity or reproducibility.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the diagnostic tests and assessment methods used, including laboratory, imaging, pathology, or other tools.

items.item_question: Are diagnostic challenges described (e.g., financial, cultural, linguistic barriers)?
items.item_question_background: Contextual barriers can delay or alter diagnosis; reporting them informs real-world applicability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any reported diagnostic difficulties, uncertainties, or barriers encountered.

items.item_question: Is diagnostic reasoning described, including alternative diagnoses considered?
items.item_question_background: Explicit reasoning allows readers to evaluate clinical logic and differential diagnosis handling.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the narrative describing clinical reasoning, differential diagnoses, and justification for final diagnosis.

items.item_question: Are prognostic characteristics described where applicable?
items.item_question_background: Prognostic factors inform expected disease course and contextualize outcomes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract reported prognostic factors or staging information, if applicable.

items.item_question: Are therapeutic intervention types described (e.g., pharmacologic, surgical, preventive)?
items.item_question_background: Identifying intervention type clarifies mechanism and scope of management strategy.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract the type(s) of intervention administered.

items.item_question: Are details of intervention administration provided (dosage, strength, duration)?
items.item_question_background: Incomplete dosing or procedural details prevent replication and translation into practice.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract dosage, route, frequency, duration, and procedural details of interventions.

items.item_question: Are changes in intervention (with rationale) described?
items.item_question_background: Adjustments to treatment reflect response and tolerance; omission hides dynamic clinical decision-making.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any modifications to therapy and the reasons provided.

items.item_question: Are clinician- and patient-assessed outcomes described?
items.item_question_background: Outcome perspectives from both clinician and patient provide a fuller understanding of effectiveness and quality of life impact.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract objective outcomes (clinical/lab measures) and patient-reported outcomes if described.

items.item_question: Are follow-up diagnostic tests and their results described?
items.item_question_background: Follow-up testing confirms response or progression; omission obscures trajectory.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract follow-up investigations and results.

items.item_question: Is intervention adherence and tolerability described?
items.item_question_background: Non-adherence and adverse tolerability influence outcome interpretation and real-world applicability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements about adherence, side effects, and tolerability.

items.item_question: Are adverse and unanticipated events described?
items.item_question_background: Unexpected harms are often the main contribution of case reports; missing them undermines safety learning.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any reported adverse or unexpected events.

items.item_question: Does the discussion address strengths and limitations of the case report?
items.item_question_background: Acknowledging limitations prevents overgeneralisation from a single case and clarifies evidentiary boundaries.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract discussion of methodological or contextual strengths and limitations.

items.item_question: Does the discussion reference relevant medical literature?
items.item_question_background: Situating the case within existing evidence distinguishes novelty from known patterns.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract citations or narrative comparisons to similar published cases or studies.

items.item_question: Does the discussion provide a scientific rationale and clear conclusions?
items.item_question_background: Explicit reasoning from observation to inference prevents unsupported speculation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract concluding statements that articulate rationale and key lessons.

items.item_question: Is the patient’s perspective or experience described?
items.item_question_background: Including the patient voice enhances understanding of lived experience and treatment impact.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract any direct quotes or narrative describing the patient’s perspective; if absent, mark Absent.
items.item_expected_location: Case presentation / Separate patient perspective section

items.item_question: Does the report state that informed consent was obtained from the patient?
items.item_question_background: Case reports involve identifiable personal information; ethical publication requires documented informed consent.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Locate and extract the informed consent statement confirming patient consent for publication.

EvidenceExamplesPositive: A rare adverse drug reaction: a case report
Matching.Explain: Routes single-patient clinical case reports to the CARE guideline. Excludes trials, observational analytic studies, and evidence syntheses.
